hook: One district is printing 6% gross yield while D10 landlords collect 3.8%

slides.title: Rental Yield Reality Check
slides.subtitle: Q1 2026 URA data reveals which districts pay landlords and which don't.

slides.headline: District 14 logged the most condo leases in Q1 2026
slides.footnote: Median 2BR monthly rent at Sims Urban Oasis, Sims Drive, D14. URA Rental Contracts Q1 2026.

slides.title: Who's Actually Making Money Renting

items.label: D25 · Woodlands
items.sub: Rosewood Suites 3BR avg $4,300/mth vs ~$850K entry price

items.label: D14 · Geylang/Aljunied
items.sub: Sims Urban Oasis 2BR avg $3,875/mth vs ~$900K entry price

items.label: D15 · Tanjong Rhu
items.sub: Tanjong Rhu Condo 2BR avg $4,250/mth vs ~$1.1M entry price

items.label: D10 · Bukit Timah
items.sub: Shangri-La Residences avg $11,200/mth but entry prices $3M+

slides.footnote: Gross yield = annualised median rent ÷ indicative entry price. URA Rental Contracts Q1 2026.

slides.headline: Wallich Residence 4BR commands Singapore's top condo rent
slides.footnote: Wallich Residence, Wallich St, D02 — 4BR 1,750 sqft lease transacted Jan 2026. URA data.

slides.title: 3 Rental Truths Landlords Must Know
items: D25 (Woodlands) 3BR rents hit $4,000–$4,600/mth — outer-region stock is punching above its weight on yield.
D14 2BR units at Sims Urban Oasis cleared consistently at $3,800–$4,150 across all three months — demand is sticky, not seasonal.
D10 prestige condos like Shangri-La Residences fetch $9,752–$14,943/mth but capital values are so high gross yields fall below 4%.
D02 Wallich Residence 1BR rents rose from $7,000 (Jan) to $7,500 (Mar) — a 7% uplift in one quarter on the smallest unit type.
Nassim Road detached house (D10) leased at $62,000/mth — landed luxury rental demand from senior expats remains resilient.

slides.title: How to Clear 5% Gross in This Market
items: Target D25 or D14 — both districts show median gross yields above 5% based on Q1 2026 URA transactions.
Buy smaller units. A D14 1BR at $3,300–$3,700/mth on a $750K purchase clears 5.3–5.9% gross before costs.
Avoid trophy-name condos in D10. Brand premium inflates purchase price faster than it lifts rent, compressing yields to sub-4%.
Watch D02 micro-units: Wallich 1BR at $7,500/mth on a ~$1.7M entry is ~5.3% gross — rare D02 yield play worth tracking.

slides.title: Yield Is Won at Purchase, Not at Lease
slides.body: Every landlord watching D10 rents thinks they're winning because the rent is big. They're not tracking the denominator. D25 and D14 buyers who entered at $800K–$950K are clearing 5–6% gross right now. That gap only closes if D10 prices correct hard or rents spike further — neither is imminent in 2026.
slides.quote: Big rent number, small yield. Small rent number, big yield. Know the difference.

caption:
📈 D25 is printing 6% gross yield while D10 landlords are stuck at 3.8%.

• Rosewood Suites (D25) 3BR avg $4,300/mth — gross yield ~6.1% on $850K entry
• Sims Urban Oasis (D14) 2BR cleared $3,875/mth consistently across Q1 2026
• Shangri-La Residences (D10) rents hit $14,943 but entry prices crush the yield

Yield is won at purchase, not at lease signing. D25 and D14 are where the math works right now.
